summary: Engagement Desk module frontend testing. Most pages load correctly. Two critical Supabase schema issues found: missing 'platform' column in engagement_categories table AND missing FK relationship for 'reviewed_by' in engagement_submissions. These cause category creation to fail and submissions/validate pages to show errors.

critical.issue: Could not find the 'platform' column of 'engagement_categories' in the schema cache. Category creation fails when platform is selected. The engagement migration SQL was never created/run.
Could not find a relationship between 'engagement_submissions' and 'reviewed_by' in the schema cache. Affects validate queue, submissions page, and dashboard team submissions list.

integration_issues.issue: Fails with Supabase error: 'platform' column not in schema. Categories without platform (None) would still fail the insert due to schema mismatch.

integration_issues.flow: Validate Queue / My Submissions / Dashboard team submissions
integration_issues.issue: Supabase query fails because engagement_submissions.reviewed_by FK relationship not in schema cache. Pages show empty state gracefully but data won't load.

test_results.sidebar_engagement_section: PASS — Engagement, Validate Queue, Categories, Eng. Settings all visible
test_results.engagement_dashboard: PASS — loads with 5 stats cards (0 values), daily target progress bar (0/50), validation queue section
test_results.validate_queue: PASS — loads, shows 0 PENDING REVIEW, queue clear message. Error in console about reviewed_by FK
test_results.categories_page: PASS — loads with 3 existing categories (ATTACK, DEFEND, EDUCATE), + NEW CATEGORY button works, modal opens
test_results.settings_page: PASS — loads with Daily Submission Target (50), Submission Expiry (24h), Max File Size (10MB), Resubmit Limit (0) fields visible
test_results.submissions_page: PASS — loads, shows MY SUBMISSIONS with empty state. Error in console about reviewed_by FK
test_results.create_category: FAIL — Supabase error: 'platform' column not found in engagement_categories schema
test_results.dashboard_empty_state: PASS — gracefully shows 0 stats when no submissions exist

rca_of_issue.root_cause: The engagement module requires new Supabase DB tables with specific columns that don't exist. No migration SQL file exists for the engagement tables. The existing tables (engagement_categories, engagement_submissions) appear to be missing columns: 'platform' in engagement_categories, and the 'reviewed_by' FK relationship in engagement_submissions pointing to profiles/users table.
rca_of_issue.reproduction: 1. Go to /engagement/admin/categories. 2. Click + NEW CATEGORY. 3. Fill name, select INSTAGRAM platform, click CREATE CATEGORY. 4. Error: 'Could not find the platform column of engagement_categories in the schema cache'
rca_of_issue.mitigation: Create and run a Supabase migration SQL to add missing columns: ALTER TABLE engagement_categories ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS platform TEXT; ALTER TABLE engagement_submissions ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS reviewed_by UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id); Then reload Supabase schema cache (or use supabase db reset).

action_items: CRITICAL: Create engagement Supabase migration SQL file. Need to add 'platform' TEXT column to engagement_categories table.
CRITICAL: Add reviewed_by UUID FK to engagement_submissions referencing auth.users or profiles table.
Run migration in Supabase Studio SQL Editor and reload schema cache.
Check if ALL engagement tables (engagement_categories, engagement_submissions, engagement_config, engagement_activity_log) have proper schema matching what the app expects.
critical_code_review_comments: No Supabase migration SQL file exists for engagement tables — /app/frontend/supabase/migrations/ only has payroll and accounting files
The app code references platform column and reviewed_by FK which don't exist in the DB schema, causing runtime failures
The 'ATTACK/DEFEND/EDUCATE' categories exist in DB but without platform column, confirming partial migration was run without all columns

success_rate.frontend: 75% (9/12 test cases passed, 2 critical DB schema issues)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Run engagement migration SQL, then retest: category creation with platform field, submissions query with reviewed_by relationship. All pages load correctly — only DB schema issues prevent category creation and data loading in submissions/validate pages.
